title: How to return a month based on unusual month start dates and end dates?

posts.author: catkins (Crystal Atkins)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi All,

I am needing to create a formula that will will return a month based on the date in another column.

Currently I have FORMAT_DATE(Today(),“MMMM”) which works great, except our revenue month starts on the 11th and ends on the 10th.

How do I compensate for this unusual month start and end in my formula?

posts.author: DipaliPatankar (Dipali Patankar)
posts.cookedHtml:
Crystal Atkins:

FORMAT_DATE(Today(),“MMMM”)

You need a conditional formula for this

Try the following using the column Date in this example. Change the column name to the column in your board

IF(Day({Date})>10,FORMAT_DATE({Date},“MMMM”),
IF(MONTH({Date})=1,“December”,FORMAT_DATE(ADD_DAYS({Date},-20),“MMMM”))
)

posts.author: catkins (Crystal Atkins)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you so much! This works perfectly for every day but the 1st lol. Do you know of any workaround for that?

posts.author: DipaliPatankar (Dipali Patankar)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Crystal

Do you mean the 1st of the month? Because its working when I tried it on my board

image299×502 16.5 KB

Could you share the date that its not working for and also an image of the board?

posts.author: catkins (Crystal Atkins)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Dipali,

Thank you so much for helping me with this. Yes, it is the first of the month that is giving me trouble. If I change it to the 2nd, it works but when it is the 1st, it returns the same month.

I have included a picture for you.

posts.author: DipaliPatankar (Dipali Patankar)
posts.cookedHtml: Can you paste the formula you used here. This seems to work correctly in my board but I may be missing something

posts.author: catkins (Crystal Atkins)
posts.cookedHtml:
Sure, here it is.

IF(Day({ACM Ship Date})>10,FORMAT_DATE({ACM Ship Date},“MMMM”),
IF(MONTH({ACM Ship Date})=1,“December”,FORMAT_DATE(ADD_DAYS({ACM Ship Date},-20),“MMMM”))
)

posts.author: DipaliPatankar (Dipali Patankar)
posts.cookedHtml:
I modified the formula so this should work

IF(Day({Date})>10, FORMAT_DATE({Date},“MMMM”),if(Month({Date})=1,“December”,FORMAT_DATE(Date(YEAR({Date}),Month({Date})-1,1),“MMMM”)))
Basically I have calculated the date in the last bit so that it is the 1st of the previous month and then extracted the month from it.

posts.author: catkins (Crystal Atkins)
posts.cookedHtml:
Here is the thread I found pertaining to this exact problem.

Month() function/formula evaluates February 1st wrong, Month(February 1) => 1 but should be 2 Platform discussions
The Formula column Month() function is supposed to evaluate to the number of the month of the year. Month(January 10) => 1 However, I get an invalid result for February 1st. I have not verified with all 364 other days of the year, but it would be nice if we could know that this evaluates correctly. [image]

I have another unusual date situation. I need the same thing from above but this time on the 15th of the month, it changes to the next month. How do I modify this formula for this situation?

posts.author: DipaliPatankar (Dipali Patankar)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dipali Patankar:

IF(Day({Date})>10, FORMAT_DATE({Date},“MMMM”),if(Month({Date})=1,“December”,FORMAT_DATE(Date(YEAR({Date}),Month({Date})-1,1),“MMMM”)))

Hi Crystal,

posts.author: skmdc (SK)
posts.cookedHtml:
@catkins @DipaliPatankar I think the hard coding of the day = 1 in the formula is probably causing the weird behavior. Please see modified formula with the change in bold,

IF(DAY({Date})>10, FORMAT_DATE({Date},“MMMM”),
FORMAT_DATE(DATE(YEAR({Date}), MONTH({Date})-1, DAY({Date})),“MMMM”))

Works for me, hope it does for you all too.

posts.author: catkins (Crystal Atkins)
posts.cookedHtml:
Sorry, here is the formula as well. I forgot to attach it in my reply.

IF(Day({Planned Completion})>15, FORMAT_DATE({Planned Completion},“MMMM”),if(Month({Planned Completion})=1,“December”,FORMAT_DATE(Date(YEAR({Planned Completion}),Month({Planned Completion})+1,DAY({Planned Completion})),“MMMM”)))

posts.author: catkins (Crystal Atkins)
posts.cookedHtml:
Actually, I think I just solved this issue with this formula. Could one of you verify on your side as well?

IF(Day({Planned Completion})<15,FORMAT_DATE({Planned Completion},“MMMM”),if(Month({Planned Completion})=1,“December”,FORMAT_DATE(Date(YEAR({Planned Completion}),Month({Planned Completion})+1,1),“MMMM”)))

image475×640 30 KB

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @catkins,

Try:

IF(Day({Planned Completion})<15,
    FORMAT_DATE({Planned Completion},"MMMM"),
    FORMAT_DATE(EDATE({Planned Completion},1),"MMMM")
)

You don’t need to figure out if the date is in December, You just add 1 month to the date (through the EDATE function) if the day is 15+.

 

What if we could break free of the Formula column? and write formulas that update any type of columns? What if a formula could update multiple columns at once? This is possible with the Advanced Formula Booster app.

posts.author: catkins (Crystal Atkins)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Gilles,

The only issue I see at the moment is with the 1st of the month but we can work around that.

I wish I could do this with an automation instead so that I can use this column in another automation. For some reason monday.com will not allow formulas to be used in automations and I cannot find an automation to do this.
